mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns are negative during rate cut periods over a 3-year rolling window with p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive equity returns as corporate revenue growth accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to immediate positive S&P 500 returns during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 during expansion regimes, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer spending capacity, creating a persistent positive drift in equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Mean forward returns are negative during months with positive payroll growth over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in 70 percent of months where monthly CPI growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and industrial production confirms broad-based economic strength, driving sustained positive equity returns as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than half of the observation periods during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling retail sales signals a demand shock and recession onset, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 65 percent of periods exhibiting this divergence pattern across a 3-year walk-forward test.
